Saturday summary: Stradbroke proves alluring for Skyhook after Hawkesbury Guineas win

Snitzel remains on track for a fifth Champion Sire title after producing a trans-Tasman juvenile stakes double on Saturday with The Next Episode and Zafar. At Bendigo, Tycoon Star's half-brother Marffiano kicked off his career on a high with victory in the $250,000 VOBIS Gold Rush.

The first GI Kentucky Oaks run under lights during primetime was won by Gunrunner filly Always A Runner, a close cousin to Lady Of Camelot, while the GI La Troienne Stakes yielded another top flight winner for Into Mischief with the Flying Dutchmen's Shred The Gnar.
A big Saturday of racing brings together emerging and proven talent all across Australia, both young and old, with the G1 South Australian Derby at Morphettville the standout.

Jane Henning has taken ownership of The Stallion Nomination Centre, ushering in a new era with a digital refresh and renewed vision, while building on the strong foundations established by well-respected long-time industry figure Brett Howard.

Sumbe and Nurlan Bizakov have strengthened their Southern Hemisphere ambitions with the appointment of Will Johnson as the Southern Hemisphere Bloodstock and Racing Manager, following a sequence of stallion, sales, and investment moves signalling deeper Australasian intent.

A nine-strong roster will stand at Vinery Stud this season, with Champion First Season Sire and Group 1 producer Ole Kirk leading the way at a new fee of $110,000 (inc GST). A Group 1-winning son of Too Darn Hot, Tropicus will debut at $27,500 (inc GST) while the stud eagerly anticipates Hawaii Five Oh's first foals going through the ring.

Henry Dwyer’s European experiment began with Asfoora. After two seasons, three elite sprint wins and a European Champion Sprinter title, it has now evolved into a Newmarket satellite stable designed to test and develop selected European purchases before they head to Australia.
